Petition For Order Directing Charles Schwab And First Republic Bank To Release Trust Funds To Trustee Of The Survivors Trust; Petition For Order Construing Trust Provisions
Off Calendar Jul-15-2014 Continued To Sep-09-14 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Carolyn Espey for Cathy Cheung, Trustee of the Survivors Trust.

Ms. Espy stated that she has spoken with Mr. Brian and Ms. Zulaica, that they are under contract for sale of the house to a third party and that they have agreed to continue the matter to August 26, 2014. The petition for order directing release of trust funds, etc. and the petition for order determining construction of trust documents were continued to September 9, 2014 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. If the sale occurs prior to that date, Ms. Espey will request that the matters be taken off calendar and will call the court in advance if more time is needed.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. McConico. (Not Reported)

Petition For Order Determining Construction Of Trust Documents
Off Calendar Jul-15-2014 Continued To Sep-09-14 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Order Directing Charles Schwab And First Republic Bank To Release Trust Funds To Trustee Of The Survivors Trust; Petition For Order Construing Trust Provisions
Off Calendar Jun-10-2014 Continued To Jul-15-14 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Yulissa Zulaica for and with Tim Zhang. Carolyn Espey for Cathy Cheung (Trustee of the Survivor's Trust). Richard Bryan for Beneficiaries of the Ted Cheung Trust

Ms. Zulaica stated that the Substitution of Attorney and a status report were filed last week, that they have spoken with Ms. Espey, that they are working together to sell the property and asked that the petitions be kept on calendar. The Petition for Order Directing Charles Schwab and First Republic Bank to Release Trust Funds to Trustee of the Survivor's Trust, etc. and the Petition for Order Determining Construction of Trust Documents were continued to July 15, 2014 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status on dismissal of the petitions.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Allen. (Not Reported)

Petition For Order Directing Charles Schwab And First Republic Bank To Release Trust Funds To Trustee Of The Survivors Trust; Petition For Order Construing Trust Provisions
Off Calendar Feb-25-2014 Continued To May-27-14 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Michael Blacksburg for Tim Zhang (Trustee of the Bypass Trust). Carolyn Espey Silverman for Cathy Cheung (Trustee of the Survivor's Trust). Richard Bryan for Beneficiaries of the Survivor's Trust.

The Parties entered into a stipulation with regard to sale of the property and distribution of the proceeds. Terms of the stipulation were stated for the record. All Counsel concur with the terms as stated. The matters were continued to May 27, 2014 at 10:30 a.m. (special set), in Dept. 204 for status of sale of the property. Any Party can request that the matter be added to calendar for issues that relate to the stipulation to sell the property on seven calendar days notice. Any papers related the hearings are to be provided to the court 24 hours in advance of the hearing date.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Court Reporter: Deborah Fuqua, CSR#12948, (415) 810-7170. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Allen. (Reported)

Petition For Order Determining Construction Of Trust Documents
Off Calendar Feb-25-2014 Continued To May-27-14 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Order Determining Construction Of Trust Documents
Off Calendar Jan-28-2014 Continued To Feb-25-14 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Order Directing Charles Schwab And First Republic Bank To Release Trust Funds To Trustee Of The Survivors Trust; Petition For Order Construing Trust Provisions
Off Calendar Jan-28-2014 Continued To Feb-25-14 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Michael Blacksburg for Tim Zhang (Trustee of the Bypass Trust). Carolyn Espey Silverman for Cathy Cheung. Richard Bryan for Beneficiaries of the Survivor's Trust.

Ms. Silverman noted language that needed to be corrected/clarified in the order of January 23, 2014 issued by the Court with regard to page 3 foot note the lower unit (Ted lived there not Tim), page 6 line 7 (Jeanie Fong is a Tenant and is unrelated to either Party) and page 4 line 7 and Page 8 lines 3 and 4 (the Survivor's Trust interest in the Cabrillo Street property should pass to the House Trust). Ms. Silverman also stated that James and Tim do not have any Children, that when she reads the Trust it is not clear on where the money goes and that she would like clarification in a order when the house sells. Mr. Bryan stated that he thinks that after the house sells half of the funds will go to the Bypass Trust and half to the Survivor's Trust, that it is not a life estate, that it is a right to use the property, that is has some value and that if there is any residual value it would go to the Cemetery Trust. Mr. Blacksburg stated that they do not have written stipulation to sell the property, that they need to determine value of their interest before the sale, and that he is not sure that sale of the property is in the best interest of the Bypass Trust and the Beneficiaries. The Petition for Order Directing Release of Trust Funds to Trustee, etc. and the Petition for Order Determining Construction of Trust Documents were continued to February 25, 2014 at 10:30 a.m., in Dept. 204 to see if Parties can enter into a stipulation to sell the property.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Court Reporter: Linda Forster, CSR#13286 (925) 548-1420. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy Allen. (Reported)

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Oral Argument On Remaining Issues
Off Calendar Jan-23-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Carolyn Espey Silverman for and with Cathy Cheung (Trustee of the Survivor's Trust). Richard Brian for and with Cathy Cheung and Lily Sathienthirakul (Beneficiaries of the Survivor's Trust). Michael Blacksburg for and with Tim Zhang (Trustee of the Bypass Trust).

Mr. Blacksburg stipulates to allow Ms. Silverman to file an amended petition to include a partition action but does not agree to the request for preliminary injunction. The underlying petitions and the amended petition are to be set for January 28, 2014. Counsel presented oral argument to support their positions with regard to the Bypass Trust's responsibilty for 50% of the line of credit, what the loan was used for (health, maintenance, support, etc.), what the intent of the instrument is, whether the restraining order puts a restriction on the sale of the property, the Survivor's Trust paying off the Bypass Trust's line of credit, who is responsible for the line of credit payback, interest on the line of credit, etc. There is no objection to the Court deciding whether the Parties have a right to partition. Mr. Blacksburg stipulates to not occupying or renting the property, and keeping the status quo until January 28, 2014. The temporary restraining order is extended until January 28, 2014 (order for the same signed in open court). The issue of the line of credit and prohibition of the sale were taken under submission.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Court Reporter: Vicki Haines, CSR#5995 (415) 676-7416. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy W. Clarke. (Reported)

Petition For Order Directing Charles Schwab And First Republic Bank To Release Trust Funds To Trustee Of The Survivors Trust; Petition For Order Construing Trust Provisions

Off Calendar Dec-23-2013 Continued To Jan-28-14 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 204

Ex Parte Application For Order To Show Cause Re Preliminary Injunction And Temporary Restraining Order
Off Calendar Dec-11-2013 Granted Per Written Order

Petition For Order Determining Construction Of Trust Documents
Off Calendar Dec-05-2013 Continued To Dec-23-13 At 11:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Michael Blacksburg for Tim Zhang (Trustee of the Bypass Trust). Carolyn Espey Silverman for and with Cathy Cheung (Trustee of the Survivor's Trust). Richard Bryan for and with Cathy Cheung and Lily Sathienthurakal (Beneficiaries of the Survibor's Trust).

Counsel confirmed that they have received the Court's Order on Bifurcated Issues which was faxed to them yesterday. Discussions were held with regard to what are the remaining issues and how to go forward. Mr. Blacksburg raised the issue that no partition action has been filed. Mr. Bryan suggested that the petition can be amended to bring partition into the action. The Court suggested that parties can stipulate to have Cathy and Lily amend the petition to include partition. Ms. Silverman wanted to present oral argument on the remaining issues. At the end of the discussion, the Court confirmed that the parties agreed to set a date to present oral argument (no witness will be called) on the following issues: 1)Line of credit and 2)Prohibition of sale. The matter is continued to December 23, 2013 at 11:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for oral argument. Mr. Blacksburg will advise the Court whether his client will stipulate to Cathy and Lily to amend the petition. The two underlying petitions ( are continued to the same date.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Court Reporter: Michelle Savage, CSR #12957 Cell: 209-662-0101. Clerk: Ella Yip. Bailiff: Deputy T. Bradley.

MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Carolyn Espey Silverman for Cathy Cheung (Trustee). Michael Blacksburg for Tim Zhang (Trustee of the Bypass Trust).

Ms. Silverman stated that an appraisal came in at 1.3 million. Mr. Blacksburg stated that they offered a buyout to the Trust Beneficiaries, that they have not heard back yet, that his Appraiser has questions regarding said appraisal and that his appraisal came in at $1,025,000.00. Ms. Silverman also stated that she e-mailed the Son of Xiu Lian Liang, that the Son indicated that Ms. Liang will sign a release, and that she will prepare a release and e- mail it. Counsel are to file trial briefs, witness list, etc. in advance of the trial date. Mr. Blacksburg stated that the issue of whether the amendments are valid needs to be addressed first. The Petition for Order Determining Construction of Trust Documents and the Petition for Order Directing Charles Schwab and First Republic Bank to Release Trust Funds, etc. were continued to November 22, 2013 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for trial.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy T. Bradley. (Not Reported)

Petition For Order Directing Charles Schwab And First Republic Bank To Release Trust Funds To Trustee Of The Survivors Trust; Petition For Order Construing Trust Provisions
Off Calendar Nov-13-2013 Continued To Nov-22-13 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Order Determining Construction Of Trust Documents
Off Calendar Oct-30-2013 Continued To Nov-13-13 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Carolyn Espey for Cathy Cheung (Trustee of the Survivor's Trust). Richard Bryan for Lily Sathienthurakul and Cathy Cheung (Beneficiaries). Michael Balcksburg for Ted Zhang (Trustee of the Bypass Trust).

Mr. Blacksburg stated that they had mediation in August, that they came up with a buyout for the bypass property and that as a sign of good faith they released one of the accounts to the Survivor's Trust. Mr. Blacksburg also stated that they have had some back and forth, that they are waiting for an appraisal and that if this fails they are ready to go to trial on November 15th and November 22nd. Ms. Espey requested that the trial dates be pushed out stating that the Appraiser needs two and a half weeks to do the appraisal. Mr. Bryan stated that the appraisal for the Bypass property was way below market value. Counsel clarified that the remaining issues are valuation of the property, whether Xiu Lian Liang has a right to occupy the premises, whether the amendments are valid, and whether Emma has authority to give a right to live on the premises. The Court asked Counsel to see if Ms. Liang is asserting any rights and to see if the Appraiser can get the appraisal done timely. The trial dates are now set for Friday November 22, 2013 at 9:00 a.m. and Thursday December 5, 2013 at 1:30 p.m. Both petitions were continued to November 13, 2013 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy Whitney. (Not Reported)

Off Calendar Aug-07-2013 Continued To Aug-21-13 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Richard Bryan for Lilly and Cathy Cheung (Beneficiaries). Carolyn Silverman for Cathy Cheung (Trustee of the Survivor's Trust).

The matter was continued to August 21, 2013 per the Probate Examiner's tentative ruling. The parties appeared and requested that the matter be called. The Court granted the request and called the matter.

Ms. Silverman stated that mediation was held on 8/2/13, that the mediation did not conclude until 5:00 pm, and that the parties were appearing today to request a continuance of the trial dates. Ms. Silverman stated that the parties had stipulated to continue the 8/9/13 and 8/16/13 trial dates to a date after 10/11/13. In addition, Ms. Silverman noted that Mr. Blacksburg would not be appearing today but was in agreement with the continuance. The Court continued the August trial dates (two half day sessions) to November 15, 2013 and November 22, 2013 at 9:00 am in Department 204. In addition, the Court set a readiness conference on October 30, 2013 at 9:00 am in Department 204.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Bailiff: Deputy Benton. (Not Reported)

MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Michael blacksburg for and with Tim Zhang. Richard Bryan for and with Cathy Cheung and for Lilly Sathienthirakul (Beneficiaries). Carolyn Espey Silverman for and with Cathy Cheung (Trustee of the Surviving Spouse Trust).

Counsel spoke of the amendments to the Trust, the Cabrillo property and what the Trustor's intent was. The Parties agreed to participate in mediation. The Court signed the order referring the matter to Pro Bono Mediation. Mediation must be completed no later than July 31, 2013. The Parties are to notify the Probate Examiner as to the outcome of mediation by August 2, 2013. Both matters were continued and the Parties are to return on August 7, 2013 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status on mediation. Trial dates of August 16, 2013 and August 23, 2013 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 are reserved (two half days).

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy M. Benton.
